title: Events, Held State, Actions, Remapping, Focus, and Devices · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved game trace contains one plausible state, timing, input, vector, rendering, collision, ownership, audio, accessibility, packaging, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in device-independent remappable action layer and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state.
Map press, release, repeat, held, analog, and one-shot behavior to named actions with testable transitions.
Store configurable action bindings, detect conflicts, offer alternatives, and avoid unmodified character-key shortcuts where inappropriate.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Event Queue
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Asteroids team handles case 1651 during a headless continuous-integration replay. Build deterministic Python evidence to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent; retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Drain or pump the bounded event queue every frame and distinguish ordered events from sampled device state.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Input Accessibility
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: asteroids-input-accessibility-1guutv" that uses case 6617 to separate discrete events from held actions while preserving remapping, focus-loss release, and device-independent intent. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Input Accessibility.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Input Accessibility into observable performance evidence. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Input Accessibility, keep Input Accessibility connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-input-accessibility-1guutv
# Competency: asteroids-input-accessibility.
# Case 6617: a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling.
# Operating constraint: keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_input_accessibility_1guutv(event_frames: int = 6, pumped_frames: int = 6, actions_bound: int = 5, duplicate_bindings: int = 0, focus_lost_released: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1guutv = "1guutv-vtuug1-6617"
    assert evidence_variant_1guutv
    evidence_axes_1guutv = (
        "player1guutv",
        "seed6617",
        "inputvtuug1",
        "frame1guutv6617",
        "state66171guutv",
        "playtestvtuug16617",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1guutv)) == 6
    if event_frames < 1 or actions_bound < 3:
        return False, actions_bound, "input-shape"
    if pumped_frames != event_frames:
        return False, pumped_frames, "event-starvation"
    if duplicate_bindings > 0:
        return False, duplicate_bindings, "binding-conflict"
    if not focus_lost_released:
        return False, actions_bound, "stuck-input"
    assert pumped_frames == event_frames
    return True, actions_bound, "remappable-input"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Input Accessibility structural check is only one gate. device-independent remappable action layer must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
